content.text:
You loved her. That's what the relief is. Two years of carrying her, always on call, always waiting — you could only do that because you loved her. The relief isn't proof you didn't want to. It's proof of how much you were holding.

*pause*

The resentment and the love were both real. They were both there at the same time. That's not a moral failure. That's just what it looks like to love someone who needed more than any one person could give.

*quieter*

What you can't fix now — the conversation that didn't happen — what would you have wanted her to know?
